chapter_title.en: Chapter on What Allah {swt}, the Almighty, Has Made Permissible and Prohibited in Marriage

text: رِوَايَةِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ مُسْلِمٍ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ: "لاَ تُنْكَحُ اِبْنَةُ اَلْأَخِ وَ لاَ اِبْنَةُ اَلْأُخْتِ عَلَى عَمَّتِهَا وَ لاَ عَلَى خَالَتِهَا إِلاَّ بِإِذْنِهِمَا وَ تُنْكَحُ اَلْعَمَّةُ وَ اَلْخَالَةُ عَلَى اِبْنَةِ اَلْأَخِ وَ اِبْنَةِ اَلْأُخْتِ بِغَيْرِ إِذْنِهِمَا ".

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.4438 - And in a narration from Muhammad ibn Muslim, from Abu Ja'far Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), who said:
"The daughter of a brother and the daughter of a sister cannot be married alongside their paternal or maternal aunt except with their permission. However, the paternal and maternal aunt can be married alongside the daughter of a brother or the daughter of a sister without requiring their permission."
